page: Amazon Web Services i4i.12xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by the Intel Xeon 8375C processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 48 virtual CPUs mapped to 24 dedicated physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The system operates at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz and includes a 54 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is a key highlight, featuring 11,250 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 28 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong performance in specific workloads. The instance achieves a Passmark CPU score of 48,290.74 and a Passmark encryption test score of 43,100.5, both placing in the top 25% tier. Geekbench image processing is also strong at 14,996.0. For language model tasks, the server processes text generation for a small 135M model at 298.89 tokens per second, which is in the top 25% tier, though performance drops to a weaker 2.76 tokens per second for a large 70B model. Most other benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Redis SET operations, fall within the average middle 50% range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i4i.12xlarge offers high resource density by bundling massive local NVMe SSD storage with a substantial memory footprint, reducing the need for external storage attachments. This hardware profile is optimized for data-intensive workloads. It is highly suitable for large-scale databases, transactional systems, data warehousing, and applications requiring high-speed local storage access combined with strong cryptographic and image processing capabilities.
description: Amazon Web Services i4i.12xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by a 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon 8375C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 48 vCPUs across 24 dedicated physical cores, 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a substantial 11,250 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 28 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong performance in CPU encryption and image processing, placing in the top 25% tier, while general compute and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the average range. The high-density local storage and memory configuration make this instance highly efficient for data-intensive applications. It is best suited for large-scale databases, transactional systems, and workloads requiring rapid local storage access.

11,250 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage bundled
